source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as a flood hath watered the earth; so shall his wrath inherit the nations, that have not sought after him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even as he turned the waters into a dry land, and the earth was made dry: and his ways were made plain for their journey: so to sinners they are stumblingblocks in his wrath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Good things were created for the good from the beginning, so for the wicked, good and evil things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The principal things necessary for the life of men, are water, fire, and iron, salt, milk, and bread of flour, and honey, and the cluster of the grape, and oil, and clothing.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All these things shall be for good to the holy, so to the sinners and the ungodly they shall be turned into evil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There are spirits that are created for vengeance, and in their fury they lay on grievous torments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In the time of destruction they shall pour out their force: and they shall appease the wrath of him that made them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fire, hail, famine, and death, all these were created for vengeance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The teeth of beasts, and scorpions, and serpents, and the sword taking vengeance upon the ungodly unto destruction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In his commandments they shall feast, and they shall be ready upon earth when need is, and when their time is come they shall not transgress his word.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore from the beginning I was resolved, and I have meditated, and thought on these things and left them in writing,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All the works of the Lord are good, and he will furnish every work in due time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It is not to be said: This is worse than that: for all shall be well approved in their time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Now therefore with the whole heart and mouth praise ye him, and bless the name of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Great labour is created for all men, and a heavy yoke is upon the children of Adam from the day of their coming out of their mother’s womb, until the day of their burial into the mother of all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their thoughts, and fears of the heart, their imagination of things to come, and the day of their end:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From him that sitteth on a glorious throne, unto him that is humbled in earth and ashes:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From him that weareth purple, and beareth the crown, even to him that is covered with rough linen: wrath, envy, trouble, unquietness, and the fear of death, continual anger, and strife,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the time of rest upon his bed, the sleep of the night changeth his knowledge.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A little and as nothing is his rest, and afterward in sleep, as in the day of keeping watch.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He is troubled in the vision of his heart, as if he had escaped in the day of battle. In the time of his safety he rose up, and wondereth that there is no fear.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Such things happen to all flesh, from man even to beast, and upon sinners are sevenfold more.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moreover, death, and bloodshed, strife, and sword, oppressions, famine, and affliction, and scourges:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All these things are created for the wicked, and for their sakes came the flood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All things that are of the earth, shall return to the earth again, and all waters shall return to the sea.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All bribery, and injustice shall be blotted out, and fidelity shall stand for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The riches of the unjust shall be dried up like a river, and shall pass away with a noise like a great thunder in rain.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: While he openeth his hands he shall rejoice: but transgressors shall pine away in the end.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The offspring of the ungodly shall not bring forth many branches, and make a noise as unclean roots upon the top of a rock.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The weed growing over every water, and at the bank of the river, shall be pulled up before all grass.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Grace is like a paradise in blessings, and mercy remaineth for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The life of a laborer that is content with what he hath, shall be sweet, and in it thou shalt find a treasure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Children, and the building of a city shall establish a name, but a blameless wife shall be counted above them both.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wine and music rejoice the heart, but the love of wisdom is above them both.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The flute and the psaltery make a sweet melody, but a pleasant tongue is above them both.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thy eye desireth favour and beauty, but more than these green sown fields.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A friend and companion meeting together in season, but above them both is a wife with her husband.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Brethren are a help in the time of trouble, but mercy shall deliver more than they.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gold and silver make the feet stand sure: but wise counsel is above them both.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Riches and strength lift up the heart: but above these is the fear of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There is no want in the fear of the Lord, and it needeth not to seek for help.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The fear of the Lord is like a paradise of blessing, and they have covered it above all glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My son, in thy lifetime be not indigent: for it is better to die than to want.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The life of him that looketh toward another man’s table is not to be counted a life: for he feedeth his soul with another man’s meat.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But a man, well instructed and taught, will look to himself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Begging will be sweet in the mouth of the unwise, but in his belly there shall burn a fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O death, how bitter is the remembrance of thee to a man that hath peace in his possessions!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To a man that is at rest, and whose ways are prosperous in all things, and that is yet able to take meat!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O death thy sentence is welcome to the man that is in need, and to him whose strength faileth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who is in a decrepit age, and that is in care about all things, and to the distrustful that loseth patience!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Fear not the sentence of death. Remember what things have been before thee, and what shall come after thee: this sentence is from the Lord upon all flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And what shall come upon thee by the good pleasure of the most High? whether ten, or a hundred, or a thousand years.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For among the dead there is no accusing of life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The children of sinners become children of abominations, and they that converse near the houses of the ungodly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The inheritance of the children of sinners shall perish, and with their posterity shall be a perpetual reproach.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The children will complain of an ungodly father, because for his sake they are in reproach.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Woe to you, ungodly men, who have forsaken the law of the most high Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And if you be born, you shall be born in malediction: and if you die, in malediction shall be your portion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All things that are of the earth, shall return into the earth: so the ungodly shall from malediction to destruction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The mourning of men is about their body, but the name of the ungodly shall be blotted out.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Take care of a good name: for this shall continue with thee, more than a thousand treasures precious and great.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A good life hath its number of days: but a good name shall continue for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My children, keep discipline in peace: for wisdom that is hid, and a treasure that is not seen, what profit is there in them both?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Better is the man that hideth his folly, than the man that hideth his wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore have a shame of these things I am now going to speak of.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For it is not good to keep all shamefacedness: and all things do not please all men in opinion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be ashamed of fornication before father and mother: and of a lie before a governor and a man in power:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of an offence before a prince, and a judge: of iniquity before a congregation and a people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of injustice before a companion and friend: and in regard to the place where thou dwellest,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of theft, and of the truth of God, and the covenant: of leaning with thy elbow over meat, and of deceit in giving and taking:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of silence before them that salute thee: of looking upon a harlot: and of turning away thy face from thy kinsman.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Turn not away thy face from thy neighbour, and of taking away a portion and not restoring.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Gaze not upon another man’s wife, and be not inquisitive after his handmaid, and approach not her bed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be ashamed of upbraiding speeches before friends: and after thou hast given, upbraid not.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Repeat not the word which thou hast heard, and disclose not the thing that is secret; so shalt thou be truly without confusion, and shalt find favour before all men: be not ashamed of any of these things, and accept no person to sin thereby:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the law of the most High, and of his covenant, and of judgment to justify the ungodly:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the affair of companions and travellers, and of the gift of the inheritance of friends:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of exactness of balance and weights, of getting much or little:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of the corruption of buying, and of merchants, and of much correction of children, and to make the side of a wicked slave to bleed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Sure keeping is good over a wicked wife.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Where there are many hands, shut up, and deliver all things in number, and weight: and put all in writing that thou givest out or receivest in.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Be not ashamed to inform the unwise and foolish, and the aged, that are judged by young men: and thou shalt be well instructed in all things, and well approved in the sight of all men living.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The father waketh for the daughter when no man knoweth, and the care for her taketh away his sleep, when she is young, lest she pass away the flower of her age, and when she is married, lest she should be hateful:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In her virginity, lest she should be corrupted, and be found with child in her father’s house: and having a husband, lest she should misbehave herself, or at the least become barren.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Keep a sure watch over a shameless daughter: lest at anytime she make thee become a laughingstock to thy enemies, and a byword in the city, and a reproach among the people, and she make thee ashamed before all the multitude.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold not everybody’s beauty: and tarry not among women.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For from garments cometh a moth, and from a woman the iniquity of a man.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For better is the iniquity of a man, than a woman doing a good turn, and a woman bringing shame and reproach.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will now remember the works of the Lord, and I will declare the things I have seen. By the words of the Lord are his works.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sun giving light hath looked upon all things, and full of the glory of the Lord is his work.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Hath not the Lord made the saints to declare all his wonderful works, which the Lord Almighty hath firmly settled to be established for his glory?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath searched out the deep, and the heart of men: and considered their crafty devices.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the Lord knoweth all knowledge, and hath beheld the signs of the world, he declareth the things that are past, and the things that are to come, and revealeth the traces of hidden things.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No thought escapeth him, and no word can hide itself from him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath beautified the glorious works of his wisdom: and he is from eternity to eternity, and to him nothing may be added,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor can he be diminished, and he hath no need of any counsellor.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: O how desirable are all his works, and what we can know is but as a spark!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All these things live, and remain for ever, and for every use all things obey him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All things are double, one against another, and he hath made nothing defective.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He hath established the good things of every one. And who shall be filled with beholding his glory?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The firmament on high is his beauty, the beauty of heaven with its glorious shew.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sun when he appeareth shewing forth at his rising, an admirable instrument, the work of the most High.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At noon he burneth the earth, and who can abide his burning heat? As one keeping a furnace in the works of heat:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The sun three times as much, burneth the mountains, breathing out fiery vapours, and shining with his beams, he blindeth the eyes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Great is the Lord that made him, and at his words he hath hastened his course.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the moon in all in her season, is for a declaration of times and a sign of the world.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the moon is the sign of the festival day, a light that decreaseth in her perfection.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The month is called after her name, increasing wonderfully in her perfection.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Being an instrument of the armies on high, shining gloriously in the firmament of heaven.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The glory of the stars is the beauty of heaven; the Lord enlighteneth the world on high.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the words of the holy one they stand in judgment, and shall never fall in their watches.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Look upon the rainbow, and bless him that made it: it is very beautiful in its brightness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It encompasseth the heaven about with the circle of its glory, the hands of the most High have displayed it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By his commandment he maketh the snow to fall apace, and sendeth forth swiftly the lightnings of his judgment.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Through this are the treasures opened, and the clouds fly out like birds.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By his greatness he hath fixed the clouds, and the hailstones are broken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At his sight shall the mountains be shaken, and at his will the south wind shall blow.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The noise of his thunder shall strike the earth, so doth the northern storm, and the whirlwind:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as the birds lighting upon the earth, he scattereth snow, and the falling thereof, is as the coming down of locusts.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The eye admireth at the beauty of the whiteness thereof, and the heart is astonished at the shower thereof.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shall pour frost as salt upon the earth: and when it freezeth, it shall become like the tops of thistles.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The cold north wind bloweth, and the water is congealed into crystal; upon every gathering together of waters it shall rest, and shall clothe the waters as a breastplate.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And it shall devour the mountains, and burn the wilderness, and consume all that is green as with fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A present remedy of all is the speedy coming of a cloud, and a dew that meeteth it, by the heat that cometh, shall overpower it.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: At his word the wind is still, and with his thought he appeaseth the deep, and the Lord hath planted islands therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let them that sail on the sea, tell the dangers thereof: and when we hear with our ears, we shall admire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There are great and wonderful works: a variety of beasts, and of all living things, and the monstrous creatures of whales.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Through him is established the end of their journey, and by his word all things are regulated.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: We shall say much, and yet shall want words: but the sum of our words is, He is all.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: What shall we be able to do to glorify him? for the Almighty himself is above all his works.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord is terrible, and exceeding great, and his power is admirable.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Glorify the Lord as much as ever you can, for he will yet far exceed, and his magnificence is wonderful.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessing the Lord, exalt him as much as you can; for he is above all praise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When you exalt him put forth all your strength, and be not weary: for you can never go far enough.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who shall see him, and declare him? and who shall magnify him as he is from the beginning?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There are many things hidden from us that are greater than these: for we have seen but a few of his works.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But the Lord hath made all things, and to the godly he hath given wisdom.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let us now praise men of renown and our fathers in their generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath wrought great glory through his magnificence from the beginning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Such as have borne rule in their dominions, men of great power, and endued with their wisdom, shewing forth in the prophets the dignity of prophets,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And ruling over the present people, and by the strength of wisdom instructing the people in most holy words.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Such as by their skill sought out musical tunes, and published canticles of the scriptures.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Rich men in virtue, studying beautifulness: living at peace in their houses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: All these have gained glory in their generations, and were praised in their days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that were born of them have left a name behind them, that their praises might be related:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there are some, of whom there is no memorial: who are perished, as if they had never been: and are become as if they had never been born, and their children with them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But these were men of mercy, whose godly deeds have not failed:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Good things continue with their seed,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their posterity are a holy inheritance, and their seed hath stood in the covenants.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their children for their sakes remain for ever: their seed and their glory shall not be forsaken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Their bodies are buried in peace, and their name liveth unto generation and generation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let the people shew forth their wisdom, and the church declare their praise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Henoch pleased God, and was translated into paradise, that he may give repentance to the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Noe was found perfect, just, and in the time of wrath he was made a reconciliation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore was there a remnant left to the earth, when the flood came.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The covenants of the world were made with him, that all flesh should no more be destroyed with the flood.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Abraham was the great father of a multitude of nations, and there was not found the like to him in glory, who kept the law of the most High, and was in covenant with him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In his flesh he established the covenant, and in temptation he was found faithful.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore by an oath he gave him glory in his posterity, that he should increase as the dust of the earth,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And that he would exalt his seed as the stars, and they should inherit from sea to sea, and from the river to the ends of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he did in like manner with Isaac for the sake of Abraham his father.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord gave him the blessing of all nations, and confirmed his covenant upon the head of Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He acknowledged him in his blessings, and gave him an inheritance, and divided him his portion in twelve tribes.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he preserved for him men of mercy, that found grace in the eyes of all flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses was beloved of God, and men: whose memory is in benediction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made him like the saints in glory, and magnified him in the fear of his enemies, and with his words he made prodigies to cease.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He glorified him in the sight of kings, and gave him commandments in the sight of his people, and shewed him his glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He sanctified him in his faith, and meekness, and chose him out of all flesh.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he heard him, and his voice, and brought him into a cloud.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he gave him commandments before his face, and a law of life and instruction, that he might teach Jacob his covenant, and Israel his judgments.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He exalted Aaron his brother, and like to himself of the tribe of Levi:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made an everlasting covenant with him, and gave him the priesthood of the nation, and made him blessed in glory,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he girded him about with a glorious girdle, and clothed him with a robe of glory, and crowned him with majestic attire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He put upon him a garment to the feet, and breeches, and an ephod, and he compassed him with many little bells of gold all round about,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That as he went there might be a sound, and a noise made that might be heard in the temple, for a memorial to the children of his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He gave him a holy robe of gold, and blue, and purple, a woven work of a wise man, endued with judgment and truth:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Of twisted scarlet the work of an artist, with precious stones cut and set in gold, and graven by the work of a lapidary for a memorial, according to the number of the tribes of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a crown of gold upon his mitre wherein was engraved Holiness, an ornament of honour: a work of power, and delightful to the eyes for its beauty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Before him there were none so beautiful, even from the beginning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No stranger was ever clothed with them, but only his children alone, and his grandchildren for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His sacrifices were consumed with fire every day.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Moses filled his hands and anointed him with holy oil.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: This was made to him for an everlasting testament, and to his seed as the days of heaven, to execute the office of the priesthood, and to have praise, and to glorify his people in his name.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He chose him out of all men living, to offer sacrifice to God, incense, and a good savour, for a memorial to make reconciliation for his people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he gave him power in his commandments, in the covenants of his judgments, that he should teach Jacob his testimonies, and give light to Israel in his law.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And strangers stood up against him, and through envy the men that were with Dathan and Abiron, compassed him about in the wilderness, and the congregation of Core in their wrath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord God saw and it pleased him not, and they were consumed in his wrathful indignation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He wrought wonders upon them, and consumed them with a flame of fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he added glory to Aaron, and gave him an inheritance, and divided unto him the firstfruits of the increase of the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He prepared them bread in the first place unto fulness: for the sacrifices also of the Lord they shall eat, which he gave to him, and to his seed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But he shall not inherit among the people in the land, and he hath no portion among the people: for he himself is his portion and inheritance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Phinees the son of Eleazar is the third in glory, by imitating him in the fear of the Lord:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he stood up in the shameful fall of the people: in the goodness and readiness of his soul he appeased God for Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore he made to him a covenant of peace, to be the prince of the sanctuary, and of his people, that the dignity of priesthood should be to him and to his seed for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And a covenant to David the king, the son of Jesse of the tribe of Juda, an inheritance to him and to his seed, that he might give wisdom into our heart to judge his people in justice, that their good things might not be abolished, and he made their glory in their nation everlasting.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Valiant in war was Jesus the son of Nave, who was successor of Moses among the prophets, who was great according to his name,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Very great for the saving the elect of God, to overthrow the enemies that rose up against them, that he might get the inheritance for Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How great glory did he gain when he lifted up his hands, and stretched out swords against the cities?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who before him hath so resisted? for the Lord himself brought the enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Was not the sun stopped in his anger, and one day made as two?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He called upon the most high Sovereign when the enemies assaulted him on every side, and the great and holy God heard him by hailstones of exceeding great force.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He made a violent assault against the nation of his enemies, and in the descent he destroyed the adversaries.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That the nations might know his power, that it is not easy to fight against God. And he followed the mighty one:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And in the days of Moses he did a work of mercy, he and Caleb the son of Jephone, in standing against the enemy, and withholding the people from sins, and appeasing the wicked murmuring.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they two being appointed, were delivered out of the danger from among the number of six hundred thousand men on foot, to bring them into their inheritance, into the land that floweth with milk and honey.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord gave strength also to Caleb, and his strength continued even to his old age, so that he went up to the high places of the land, and his seed obtained it for an inheritance:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That all the children of Israel might see, that it is good to obey the holy God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then all the judges, every one by name, whose heart was not corrupted: who turned not away from the Lord,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That their memory might be blessed, and their bones spring up out of their place,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And their name continue for ever, the glory of the holy men remaining unto their children.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Samuel the prophet of the Lord, the beloved of the Lord his God, established a new government, and anointed princes over his people.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the law of the Lord he judged the congregation, and the God of Jacob beheld, and by his fidelity he was proved a prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he was known to be faithful in his words, because he saw the God of light:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And called upon the name of the Lord Almighty, in fighting against the enemies who beset him on every side, when he offered a lamb without blemish.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the Lord thundered from heaven, and with a great noise made his voice to be heard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he crushed the princes of the Tyrians, and all the lords of the Philistines:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And before the time of the end of his life in the world, he protested before the Lord, and his anointed: money, or any thing else, even to a shoe, he had not taken of any man, and no man did accuse him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And after this he slept, and he made known to the king, and shewed him the end of his life, and he lifted up his voice from the earth in prophecy to blot out the wickedness of the nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then Nathan the prophet arose in the days of David.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as the fat taken away from the flesh, so was David chosen from among the children of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He played with lions as with lambs: and with bears he did in like manner as with the lambs of the flock, in his youth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Did not he kill the giant, and take away reproach from his people?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In lifting up his hand, with the stone in the sling he beat down the boasting of Goliath:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he called upon the Lord the Almighty, and he gave strength in his right hand, to take away the mighty warrior, and to set up the horn of his nation.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So in ten thousand did he glorify him, and praised him in the blessings of the Lord, in offering to him a crown of glory:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he destroyed the enemies on every side, and extirpated the Philistines the adversaries unto this day: he broke their horn for ever.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In all his works he gave thanks to the holy one, and to the most High, with words of glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With his whole heart he praised the Lord, and loved God that made him: and he gave him power against his enemies:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he set singers before the altar, and by their voices he made sweet melody.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And to the festivals he added beauty, and set in order the solemn times even to the end of his life, that they should praise the holy name of the Lord, and magnify the holiness of God in the morning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord took away his sins, and exalted his horn for ever: and he gave him a covenant of the kingdom, and a throne of glory in Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: After him arose up a wise son, and for his sake he cast down all the power of the enemies.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Solomon reigned in days of peace, and God brought all his enemies under him, that he might build a house in his name, and prepare a sanctuary for ever: O how wise wast thou in thy youth!

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou wast filled as a river with wisdom, and thy soul covered the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou didst multiply riddles in parables: thy name went abroad to the islands far off, and thou wast beloved in thy peace.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The countries wondered at thee for thy canticles, and proverbs, and parables, and interpretations,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And at the name of the Lord God, whose surname is, God of Israel.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou didst gather gold as copper, and didst multiply silver as lead,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou didst bow thyself to women: and by thy body thou wast brought under subjection.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast stained thy glory, and defiled thy seed so as to bring wrath upon thy children, and to have thy folly kindled,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That thou shouldst make the kingdom to be divided, and out of Ephraim a rebellious kingdom to rule.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: But God will not leave off his mercy, and he will not destroy, nor abolish his own works, neither will he cut up by the roots the offspring of his elect: and he will not utterly take away the seed of him that loveth the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Wherefore he gave a remnant to Jacob, and to David of the same stock.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Solomon had an end with his fathers.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he left behind him of his seed, the folly of the nation,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Even Roboam that had little wisdom, who turned away the people through his counsel:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Jeroboam the son of Nabat, who caused Israel to sin, and shewed Ephraim the way of sin, and their sins were multiplied exceedingly.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They removed them far away from their land.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they sought out all iniquities, till vengeance came upon them, and put an end to all their sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And Elias the prophet stood up, as a fire, and his word burnt like a torch.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He brought a famine upon them, and they that provoked him in their envy, were reduced to a small number, for they could not endure the commandments of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By the word of the Lord he shut up the heaven, and he brought down fire from heaven thrice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thus was Elias magnified in his wondrous works. And who can glory like to thee?

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who raisedst up a dead man from below, from the lot of death, by the word of the Lord God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who broughtest down kings to destruction, and brokest easily their power in pieces, and the glorious from their bed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who heardest judgment in Sina, and in Horeb the judgments of vengeance.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who anointedst kings to penance, and madest prophets successors after thee.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who wast taken up in a whirlwind of fire, in a chariot of fiery horses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Who art registered in the judgments of times to appease the wrath of the Lord, to reconcile the heart of the father to the son, and to restore the tribes of Jacob.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed are they that saw thee, and were honoured with thy friendship.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For we live only in our life, but after death our name shall not be such.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Elias was indeed covered with the whirlwind, and his spirit was filled up in Eliseus: in his days he feared not the prince, and no man was more powerful than he.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No word could overcome him, and after death his body prophesied.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In his life he did great wonders, and in death he wrought miracles.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For all this the people repented not, neither did they depart from their sins till they were cast out of their land, and were scattered through all the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And there was left but a small people, and a prince in the house of David.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Some of these did that which pleased God: but others committed many sins.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Ezechias fortified his city, and brought in water into the midst thereof, and he digged a rock with iron, and made a well for water.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In his days Sennacherib came up, and sent Rabsaces, and lifted up his hand against them, and he stretched out his hand against Sion, and became proud through his power.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then their hearts and hands trembled, and they were in pain as women in travail.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And they called upon the Lord who is merciful, and spreading their hands, they lifted them up to heaven: and the holy Lord God quickly heard their voice.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He was not mindful of their sins, neither did he deliver them up to their enemies, but he purified them by the hand of Isaias, the holy prophet.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He overthrew the army of the Assyrians, and the angel of the Lord destroyed them.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For Ezechias did that which pleased God, and walked valiantly in the way of David his father, which Isaias, the great prophet, and faithful in the sight of God, had commanded him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In his days the sun went backward, and he lengthened the king’s life.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: With a great spirit he saw the things that are to come to pass at last, and comforted the mourners in Sion.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He showed what should come to pass for ever, and secret things before they came.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The memory of Josias is like the composition of a sweet smell made by the art of a perfumer:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: His remembrance shall be sweet as honey in every mouth, and as music at a banquet of wine.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He was directed by God unto the repentance of the nation, and he took away the abominations of wickedness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he directed his heart towards the Lord, and in the days of sinners he strengthened godliness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Except David, and Ezechias and Josias, all committed sin.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For the kings of Juda forsook the law of the most High, and despised the fear of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: So they gave their kingdom to others, and their glory to a strange nation,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They burnt the chosen city of holiness, and made the streets thereof desolate according to the prediction of Jeremias.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For they treated him evil, who was consecrated a prophet from his mother’s womb, to overthrow, and pluck up, and destroy, and to build again, and renew.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: It was Ezechiel that saw the glorious vision, which was shewn him upon the chariot of cherubims.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For he made mention of the enemies under the figure of rain, and of doing good to them that shewed right ways.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And may the bones of the twelve prophets spring up out of their place: for they strengthened Jacob, and redeemed themselves by strong faith.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How shall we magnify Zorobabel? for he was as a signet on the right hand;

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In like manner Jesus the son of Josedec who in their days built the house, and set up a holy temple to the Lord, prepared for everlasting glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And let Nehemias be a long time remembered, who raised up for us our walls that were cast down, and set up the gates and the bars, who rebuilt our houses.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: No man was born upon earth like Henoch: for he also was taken up from the earth.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Nor as Joseph, who was a man born prince of his brethren, the support of his family, the ruler of his brethren, the stay of the people:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And his bones were visited, and after death they prophesied.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Seth and Sem obtained glory among men: and above every soul Adam in the beginning.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Simon the high priest, the son of Onias, who in his life propped up the house, and in his days fortified the temple.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: By him also the height of the temple was founded, the double building and the high walls of the temple.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: In his days the wells of water flowed out, and they were filled as the sea above measure.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He took care of his nation, and delivered it from destruction.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He prevailed to enlarge the city, and obtained glory in his conversation with the people: and enlarged the entrance of the house and the court.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He shone in his days as the morning star in the midst of a cloud, and as the moon at the full.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as the sun when it shineth, so did he shine in the temple of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as the rainbow giving light in bright clouds, and as the flower of roses in the days of the spring, and as the lilies that are on the brink of the water, and as the sweet smelling frankincense in the time of summer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As a bright fire, and frankincense burning in the fire.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As a massy vessel of gold, adorned with every precious stone.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: As an olive tree budding forth, and a cypress tree rearing itself on high, when he put on the robe of glory, and was clothed with the perfection of power.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When he went up to the holy altar, he honoured the vesture of holiness.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And when he took the portions out of the hands of the priests, he himself stood by the altar. And about him was the ring of his brethren: and as the cedar planted in mount Libanus,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And as branches of palm trees, they stood round about him, and all the sons of Aaron in their glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the oblation of the Lord was in their hands, before all the congregation of Israel: and finishing his service, on the altar, to honour the offering of the most high King,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He stretched forth his hand to make a libation, and offered of the blood of the grape.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: He poured out at the foot of the altar a divine odour to the most high Prince.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then the sons of Aaron shouted, they sounded with beaten trumpets, and made a great noise to be heard for a remembrance before God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then all the people together made haste, and fell down to the earth upon their faces, to adore the Lord their God, and to pray to the Almighty God the most High.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the singers lifted up their voices, and in the great house the sound of sweet melody was increased.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And the people in prayer besought the Lord the most High, until the worship of the Lord was perfected, and they had finished their office.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Then coming down, he lifted up his hands over all the congregation of the children of Israel, to give glory to God with his lips, and to glory in his name:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And he repeated his prayer, willing to shew the power of God.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And now pray ye to the God of all, who hath done great things in all the earth, who hath increased our days from our mother’s womb, and hath done with us according to his mercy.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: May he grant us joyfulness of heart, and that there be peace in our days in Israel for ever:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: That Israel may believe that the mercy of God is with us, to deliver us in his days.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: There are two nations which my soul abhorreth: and the third is no nation: which I hate:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They that sit on mount Seir, and the Philistines, and the foolish people that dwell in Sichem.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Jesus the son of Sirach, of Jerusalem, hath written in this book the doctrine of wisdom and instruction, who renewed wisdom from his heart.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Blessed is he that is conversant in these good things and he that layeth them up in his heart, shall be wise always.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For if he do them, he shall be strong to do all things: because the light of God guideth his steps.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: A prayer of Jesus the son of Sirach. I will give glory to thee, O Lord, O King, and I will praise thee, O God my Saviour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will give glory to thy name: for thou hast been a helper and protector to me.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And hast preserved my body from destruction, from the snare of an unjust tongue, and from the lips of them that forge lies, and in the sight of them that stood by, thou hast been my helper.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou hast delivered me, according to the multitude of the mercy of thy name, from them that did roar, prepared to devour.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Out of the hands of them that sought my life, and from the gates of afflictions, which compassed me about:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the oppression of the flame which surrounded me, and in the midst of the fire I was not burnt.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: From the depth of the belly of hell, and from an unclean tongue, and from lying words, from an unjust king, and from a slanderous tongue:

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My soul shall praise the Lord even to death.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And my life was drawing near to hell beneath.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: They compassed me on every side, and there was no one that would help me. I looked for the succour of men, and there was none.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I remembered thy mercy, O Lord, and thy works, which are from the beginning of the world.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: How thou deliverest them that wait for thee, O Lord, and savest them out of the hands of the nations.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Thou hast exalted my dwelling place upon the earth and I have prayed for death to pass away.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I called upon the Lord, the father of my Lord, that he would not leave me in the day of my trouble, and in the time of the proud without help.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I will praise thy name continually, and will praise it with thanksgiving, and my prayer was heard.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And thou hast saved me from destruction, and hast delivered me from the evil time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Therefore I will give thanks, and praise thee, and bless the name of the Lord.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: When I was yet young, before I wandered about, I sought for wisdom openly in my prayer.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I prayed for her before the temple, and unto the very end I will seek after her, and she flourished as a grape soon ripe.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My heart delighted in her, my foot walked in the right way, from my youth up I sought after her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I bowed down my ear a little, and received her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I found much wisdom in myself, and profited much therein.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: To him that giveth me wisdom, will I give glory.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: For I have determined to follow her: I have had a zeal for good, and shall not be confounded.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My soul hath wrestled for her, and in doing it I have been confirmed.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I stretched forth my hands on high, and I bewailed my ignorance of her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I directed my soul to her, and in knowledge I found her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I possessed my heart with her from the beginning: therefore I shall not be forsaken.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: My entrails were troubled in seeking her: therefore shall I possess a good possession.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: The Lord hath given me a tongue for my reward: and with it I will praise him.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Draw near to me, ye unlearned, and gather yourselves together into the house of discipline.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Why are ye slow and what do you say of these things? your souls are exceeding thirsty.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: I have opened my mouth, and have spoken: buy her for yourselves without silver,

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: And submit your neck to the yoke, and let your soul receive discipline: for she is near at hand to be found.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Behold with your eyes how I have laboured a little, and have found much rest to myself.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Receive ye discipline as a great sum of money, and possess abundance of gold by her.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Let your soul rejoice in his mercy and you shall not be confounded in his praise.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner

text: Work your work before the time, and he will give you your reward in his time.

source.translator: English College at Douay/Rheims; revised by Bishop Richard Challoner
